perturbation.original_text: NIST shall keep the information confidential and shall use the Proprietary Information only for internal experimental purposes or evaluation of the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.changed_text: NIST shall keep the information confidential and shall use the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'only for internal experimental purposes or evaluation of the Proprietary Information', NIST's permitted use of the Proprietary Information is broadened without clear limitations. This creates uncertainty, especially since Section 8 lists exceptions to NIST's obligations, but those exceptions don't cover expanded usage. This change contradicts the implied restriction on use that was initially present, potentially opening NIST up to unintended liabilities or uses of the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement imposes no obligation upon NIST with respect to information which (a) was in NIST ’s possession before receipt from Discloser; (b) is or becomes a matter of public knowledge through no fault of NIST; (c) is received by NIST from a third party without a duty of confidentiality; (d) is disclosed by Discloser to a third party without a duty of confidentiality on the third party; (e) is disclosed by NIST with Discloser ’s prior written approval or (f) is developed by NIST wit hout reference to the Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited NIST's use of Proprietary Information to 'internal experimental purposes or evaluation'. The changed text removes this limitation, broadening NIST's permitted use. This contradicts the listed exceptions in the 'Contradicted Text' (a through f), which do not account for or limit this now broader usage. While the 'Contradicted Text' specifies circumstances where NIST has no obligation regarding the information, it doesn't address or mitigate the broader usage rights granted by the modified clause. This creates a clear contradiction because the broadened use is not constrained by the exceptions, leading to uncertainty about the intended limitations on NIST's use of the Proprietary Information, aligning with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: NIST shall only have a duty to protect the Proprietary Information which is disclosed to it in writing and is identified as “confidential ” by Discloser, or, if disclosed orally or in any other manner, if Discloser provides NIST with a written memorandum summarizing the Proprietary Information and designating such summary as “confidential ” within thirty (30) days of the disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: NIST shall only have a duty to protect the Proprietary Information which is disclosed to it in writing and is identified as “confidential ” by Discloser.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the section specifying the terms for disclosing information other than in writing. This omission creates ambiguity and a contradiction because Proprietary Information could be disclosed in meetings but wouldn't necessarily fall under the agreement's protections, this is specially relevant since in section 1 is mentioned 'Proprietary Information : [List the names of the NIST Employees].'
perturbation.contradicted_text: The information disclosed by Discloser to NIST under this Agreement will be considered information (“Proprietary Information”). The following NIST ’s representative (s) will be receiving Proprietary Information : [List the names of the NIST Employees]. NIST shall not disclose the Proprietary Information to any of its employees other than those who have a need to review it and which employees are legally obligated to honor the confidentiality provisions herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies how proprietary information disclosed orally or in other manners (besides writing) can still be protected if summarized in writing within 30 days. The changed text removes this provision, limiting protection to only information disclosed in writing. The contradicted text states that information disclosed by the Discloser to NIST will be considered Proprietary Information and that NIST representatives will be receiving it. Since the changed text removes the allowance for orally disclosed information to be protected, it contradicts the contradicted text because it creates a scenario where information disclosed orally to the NIST representatives wouldn't necessarily be protected, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition where essential language is removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement imposes no obligation upon NIST with respect to information which (a) was in NIST ’s possession before receipt from Discloser; (b) is or becomes a matter of public knowledge through no fault of NIST; (c) is received by NIST from a third party without a duty of confidentiality; (d) is disclosed by Discloser to a third party without a duty of confidentiality on the third party; (e) is disclosed by NIST with Discloser ’s prior written approval or (f) is developed by NIST wit hout reference to the Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement imposes no obligation upon NIST with respect to information which (a) was in NIST ’s possession before receipt from Discloser; (b) is or becomes a matter of public knowledge through no fault of NIST; (c) is received by NIST from a third party without a duty of confidentiality; (d) is disclosed by Discloser to a third party without a duty of confidentiality on the third party; (e) is disclosed by NIST with Discloser ’s prior written approval.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the exemption for information 'developed by NIST without reference to the Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder', the contract now implies that NIST is obligated to protect information it independently develops, even if it's similar to the Discloser's information. This contradicts the principle that independent development shouldn't be restricted by an NDA and creates an illogical expansion of NIST's obligations. This is directly related to section 2, where its stated: 'NIST shall keep the information confidential and shall use the Proprietary Information.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: NIST shall keep the information confidential and shall use the Proprietary Information. NIST shall not make any copies of the Proprietary Information except as necessary for its employees who are entitled to review it under Section 1 above. Any copies which are made shall be identified as belonging to Discloser and marked as “confidential .”
perturbation.justification: The original text included an exemption for information developed by NIST without reference to the Discloser's Proprietary Information. This exemption was removed in the changed text. The contradicted text states 'NIST shall keep the information confidential and shall use the Proprietary Information.' By removing the exemption, the changed text now implies that NIST is obligated to protect information it independently develops, even if it's similar to the Discloser's information. This creates a direct conflict because NIST would now be obligated to keep its own, independently developed information confidential, which contradicts the principle of independent development not being restricted. This creates a strong contradiction, as NIST's obligation is significantly broadened.

perturbation.original_text: These provisions are consistent with and do not supersede, conflict with, or otherwise alter the employee obligations, rights, or liabilities created by existing statute or Executive order relating to NIST EMPLOYEE -- Please route this NDA with an NDA Memo for review and signatures. When fully signed, please forward a copy of Memo and NDA to the Technology Partnerships Office (TPO) at Mail Stop 2200. Please contact TPO if you have questio ns at (301) 975 -2573.
perturbation.changed_text: These provisions are consistent with and do not supersede, conflict with, or otherwise alter the employee obligations, rights, or liabilities created by existing statute or Executive order relating to NIST EMPLOYEE.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the routing instructions 'Please route this NDA with an NDA Memo for review and signatures. When fully signed, please forward a copy of Memo and NDA to the Technology Partnerships Office (TPO) at Mail Stop 2200. Please contact TPO if you have questio ns at (301) 975 -2573.', there is no clear process to ensure the NDA is executed and managed correctly. This creates contradiction since previous sections such as the 1st, mentions specific NIST employees who will be receiving information, where there is an absence of process on following up on NIST employee obligations, rights and liabilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The information disclosed by Discloser to NIST under this Agreement will be considered information (“Proprietary Information”). The following NIST ’s representative (s) will be receiving Proprietary Information : [List the names of the NIST Employees]. NIST shall not disclose the Proprietary Information to any of its employees other than those who have a need to review it and which employees are legally obligated to honor the confidentiality provisions herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided routing instructions for the NDA and memo. The changed text removes these instructions, creating an absence of process on following up on NIST employee obligations, rights and liabilities. This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' section, which mentions specific NIST employees receiving Proprietary Information and their obligation to honor confidentiality. By removing the routing instructions, there is no clear process to ensure these employees are made aware of their obligations or for tracking compliance, creating a contradiction between the intended confidentiality and the absence of enforcement mechanism.
